title: I Stood With The Dead
content:
I stood with the Dead, so forsaken and still:
     When dawn was grey I stood with the Dead.
    And my slow heart said, "You must kill; you must kill:
     Soldier, soldier, morning is red."

    On the shapes of the slain in their crumpled disgrace
     I stared for a while through the thin cold rain....
    "O lad that I loved, there is rain on your face,
     And your eyes are blurred and sick like the plain."

    I stood with the Dead.... They were dead; they were dead;
     My heart and my head beat a march of dismay;
    And gusts of the wind came dulled by the guns....
     "Fall in!" I shouted; "Fall in for your pay!"
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Siegfried Loraine Sassoon delivers a powerful performance in "I Stood With The Dead"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
